title: Unable to create cgroup
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to run [slurmd](https://slurm.schedmd.com/slurmd.html) inside a container, which creates cgroups. Slurmd is launched by systemd, and `systemctl status slurmd` shows:
```
error: common_cgroup_instantiate: unable to create cgroup '/sys/fs/cgroup/freezer/slurm' : Read-only file system
error: unable to build slurm cgroup for ns freezer: Read-only file system
```

I can fix the read-only error by mounting `/sys/fs/cgroup` into the container as `rw` , in which case I get the following:

```
error: common_cgroup_instantiate: unable to create cgroup '/sys/fs/cgroup/freezer/slurm' : Permission denied
error: unable to build slurm cgroup for ns freezer: Permission denied
error: unable to create slurm freezer xcgroup
```

This is on RockyLinux 8, where I know podman uses cgroups v1 (`info` output below). Podman is running as a non-root user.

I suspect that this is just not a supported configuration; there are various issues suggesting the options I've listed below might fix it but I'm not sure which combination of things are necessary and so far I haven't been able to find a working set - though I haven't tried 1 and 2 below yet as those are harder in the problem's context, I think:
1. Using cgroups v2
2. Running podman as root
3. Setting privileged
4. Mounting `/sys/fs/cgroup` into the container as `rw`
5. Unmasking `/sys/fs/cgroup`
6. Using `--cgroupns=private`
7. Maybe some of the other cgroups related options for podman or podman-run?

Is there a succinct description of what is necessary to make cgroup creation possible inside a container please? If it is just 1., or just 2. or both or whatever fine, that's useful to know (and doens't appear to be documented).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

This is quite hard to minimise as the slurmd needs a working slurmctld as well. The failures are occuring when using ansible's molecule test framework so an total reproducer on a RL8 system would be:

```shell
sudo dnf install -y podman python39 podman-plugins git
git clone https://github.com/stackhpc/ansible-role-openhpc.git
git checkout feat/update-slurmconf
python3.9 -m venv venv
. venv/bin/activate
pip install -U pip
pip install -r molecule/requirements.txt
ansible-galaxy collection install containers.podman:>=1.10.1
MOLECULE_IMAGE=rockylinux:8.7 MOLECULE_NO_LOG="false" molecule converge -s test5
MOLECULE_NO_LOG="false" MOLECULE_IMAGE=rockylinux:8.8 molecule login -s test5 --host testohpc-compute-0

[root@testohpc-compute-0 /]# systemctl status slurmd
```


### Describe the results you received

See description above

### Describe the results you expected

See description above

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
rootless users are not able to directly use cgroup v1,  you could manually set it up and chown the cgroups then as rootless use `--cgroup-parent` but it is not a configuration we support.

To avoid the problem, you either need to run as root or move to cgroup v2.

Please be aware that cgroup delegation is always unsafe on cgroup v1, even for root.
